title: Honor Spend Category lifecycle
description: Preserve stable LEDGER Spend Category identity while rejecting invalid new or activated plan intent. Implements G2 and G5 through UC-BUDGET-001, UC-BUDGET-002, and UC-BUDGET-004.
rationale: Category rename, archive, and reactivation must not rewrite history or silently revive planned amounts.
story_want: budget entries to follow the canonical Spend Category lifecycle
story_so_that: current plans remain valid and historical intent stays attributable
acceptance_criteria: Given plan validation, when BUDGET resolves a Spend Category, then it uses the versioned public LEDGER category contract and the stable category identifier rather than a display name.
Given a referenced Spend Category is renamed, when any existing revision is read, then the stored category identifier and amount remain unchanged.
Given a referenced Spend Category is archived, when historical plan state is read, then its entries remain readable and are marked with current inactive lifecycle evidence.
Given an unknown, inactive, or archived Spend Category, when a new draft entry targets it, then BUDGET rejects the complete draft before mutation.

Given a Draft references a category that became unknown, inactive, or archived after draft creation, when activation is requested, then BUDGET rejects the complete activation and preserves the former active state.

Given an archived Spend Category is reactivated, when a later draft targets its stable identifier, then the new entry may be accepted but no prior amount is copied, restored, or activated automatically.
Given only a category display name and no stable identifier, when draft creation is requested, then BUDGET rejects the request before mutation.
